text:
Market today: AI spending momentum is the dominant driver for your names today. Big funds pouring into AI (UAE's $49B raise) and continued enterprise AI buildout keep the risk-on bid alive under chips and cloud. No macro shock in today's tape - rates fear is hitting gold/commodities, not tech directly.

Your holdings: All seven positions are HOLD or NEW with no scanner sell flags - actually clean across the board. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF - margin on top of leverage means a bad day hits you hard; stop at $200 is wide, worth tightening if you're nervous. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF, barely off entry at +1.9% - tiny wiggle room before $21.38 stop triggers. Watch both closely.

Watch: 1) NVDA price action - bellwether for your whole book. 2) Any macro comment on rates that spooks growth names.

Bottom line: Your book is green and clean - protect the leveraged positions with tight attention, don't add risk chasing here.
plays: SOXL: Already long +4.8% - consider tightening stop above $200 toward $230-240 range; 3x leverage plus your margin means a gap down hits twice as hard.
SPAL: Brand-new volatile leveraged position - honor the $21.38 stop strictly, no exceptions; too early to add size.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but no single name has a catalyst strong enough to justify adding more leveraged tech exposure on an already concentrated margin book.
